title: Improved particle agglutination assay
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for determining the presence or absence of an analyte in a sample, the method involving: mixing a sample obtained from a subject with a particle-containing reagent, to form a mixture; incubating the mixture for a first interval of time; during which time the mixture may either be continually agitated or the mixture may be briefly agitated after the first interval of time has passed; acquiring an optical property of the mixture either during the interval where the mixture is being continually agitated or at the end of the first interval once the mixture has been agitated, from which a signal indicative of the analyte content of the sample is determined.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method for the determination of the presence of an analyte in a sample, comprising the steps of: a) mixing a sample obtained from a subject with a particle-containing reagent, to form a mixture; b) incubating the mixture for a first interval of time; c) continually agitating the mixture during the first interval of time or agitating the mixture after the first interval of time and acquiring an optical property of the mixture; and d) determining a signal indicative of the analyte content of the sample based on the optical property of the mixture. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein the step of acquiring an optical property comprises determining an absorbance, transmittance, reflectance, light scatter, fluorescence, or scintillation value. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2 wherein when the mixture is continually agitated following combining the sample with the particle containing-reagent, optical property values are acquired during an interval, TC1. 4. The method of claim 1 or claim 2 wherein when the mixture is agitated after the first interval of time, optical property values may be acquired during interval T1 , T2, T3 or T4, wherein T1 represents a time from the combining of sample with particle-containing reagent and before initiation of agitation of the mixture; T2 represents a time after T1 and before initiation of agitation; T3 represents a time after initiation of agitation and T4 represents a time after T3. 5. The method of any preceding claim wherein the first interval of time is at least 15 seconds, at least 30 seconds, at least 45 seconds, at least 1 minute, at least 2 minutes, at least 5 minutes, at least 10 minutes, at least 15 minutes, at least 30 minutes. 6. The method of any preceding claim wherein agitation of the mixture comprises stirring with a rod, stirring with a magnetic puck, stirring with a rotor, flowing the mixture through a nozzle, applying ultrasonic energy, vortexing or shaking the mixture. 7. The method of any preceding claim wherein the analyte is a protein. 8. The method of any preceding claim wherein the analyte is an antibody. 9. The method of any preceding claim wherein the analyte is an autoantibody. 10. The method of claim 9 wherein the autoantibody is an antibody against a citrullinated peptide or a cyclic citrullinated peptide (CCP). 11. The method of any one of claims 1-6wherein the analyte is a peptide or fragment thereof. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-6 wherein the analyte is a polynucleotide or fragment thereof. 13. The method of any preceding claim wherein acquiring an optical property of the mixture comprises acquiring one or more than one measurements of the optical property, during or after agitation of the mixture. 14. The method of any preceding claim wherein acquiring an optical property of the mixture comprises acquiring more than one measurements of the optical property, before, during or after agitation of the mixture, wherein at least one measurement is acquired prior to agitation and at least one measurement is acquired during or after agitation of the mixture 15. The method of claim 13 or 14 wherein acquiring more than one measurements of the optical property comprises acquiring data at a frequency of at least 0.01 Hz, at least 0.05 Hz, at least 0.1 Hz, at least 1 Hz, at least 3 Hz, at least 5 Hz, at least 10 Hz, at least 15 Hz, at least 30 Hz, at least 60 Hz, at least 120 Hz. 16. The method of claim 13 or 14 wherein measurement values are obtained at least 0.1 seconds, at least 0.5 seconds, at least 1 seconds, at least 2 seconds, at least 3 seconds, at least 5 seconds, at least 10 seconds, at least 15 seconds, at least 30 seconds, at least 60 seconds, at least 120 seconds, at least 240 seconds, at least 360 seconds, at least 500 seconds after initiation of agitation of the mixture or after cessation of agitation of the mixture. 17. The method of any preceding claim wherein determining a signal indicative of the analyte content comprises; comparing the at least one optical property value with that of standard value for said analyte and obtaining an analyte quantity value therefrom. 18. The method of any preceding claim wherein determining a signal indicative of the analyte content comprises; comparing the at least one optical property value against a predetermined threshold value for said analyte and assigning positive or negative outcome therefrom. 19. The method of claim 13 or 14 when dependent on claim 3, wherein determining a signal indicative of the analyte content comprises obtaining one or more measurements during interval TC1 and determining an analyte value therefrom or a positive or negative outcome therefrom. 20. The method of claim 13 or 14 when dependent on claim 4, wherein determining a signal indicative of the analyte content comprises obtaining one or more measurements during interval T3 and determining an analyte value therefrom or a positive or negative outcome therefrom. 21. The method of claim 13 or 14 when dependent on claim 4, wherein determining a signal indicative of the analyte content comprises comparing one or more measurements during interval T1 with one or more measurements during interval T3, and determining an analyte value or a positive or negative outcome therefrom. 22. The method of claim 13 or 14 when dependent on claim 4, wherein determining a signal indicative of the analyte content comprises comparing one or more measurements during interval T2 with one or more measurements during interval T3, and determining an analyte value or a positive or negative outcome therefrom. 23. The method of claim 13 or 14 when dependent on claim 4, wherein determining a signal indicative of the analyte content comprises comparing one or more measurements during interval T3 with one or more measurements during interval T4, and determining an analyte value or a positive or negative outcome therefrom. 24. The method of any one of claims 21-23 wherein the step of comparing values from one interval with values from another interval comprises subtracting measurement values 

 from one interval from measurement values from the other interval or dividing measurement values from one interval with measurement values from the other interval. 25. A homogeneous particle assay method having reduced susceptibility to non-specific binding, comprising: incubating a sample with a particle reagent comprising at least one binding pair member for the analyte of interest whilst monitoring an optical property value of the mixture and obtaining one or more optical property values of the mixture; incubating the particle containing mixture so as to form aggregated particles due to interaction of a target of interest in the sample, if present, with its binding pair member on the particle; agitating the sample to disrupt aggregated particles and continuing to monitor the optical property of the mixture and obtaining further optical property values of the mixture; determining the difference between the optical property values obtained before agitation of the mixture and the optical property values obtained after agitation of the mixture; and determining the amount of target based on the difference in the optical property values. 26. The assay method of claim 25 wherein the step of agitation preferentially disrupts non- specifically aggregated particles, thereby resulting in a greater change in optical property value compared with particles aggregated as a result of the binding of target with binding pair member on the particle. 27. The assay method of claims 25 or 26, wherein the assay is directed to an antibody potentially present in the sample. 28. The assay method of claim 27 wherein the antibody is an autoantibody. 29. The assay method of claim 28 wherein the autoantibody is an anti-citrullinated peptide or an anti-cyclic citrullinated peptide (aCCP) antibody. 30. The assay method of claims 25, wherein the assay is directed to an antigen potentially present in the sample. 31. The assay method of claim 30, wherein the antigen comprises a protein, a peptide or fragment thereof, a polynucleotide or fragment thereof, a carbohydrate, a lipid, a lipopolysaccharide. 32. The assay method of claim 28, wherein the autoantibody is an antibody-against gliadin, cardiolipin, beta 2 glycoprotein 1 , DNA, thyroid peroxidase, thyroglobulin, Intrinsic Factor, Insulin, or histone.
